# FLOW SEO Full Context


<!-- Source: docs/00-START-HERE.md -->

---
title: "Start Here"
description: "Start Here"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - flow
  - start
---

# Start Here

## What This Is

Use FLOW as a public SEO operating system: Find the demand, Leverage the places where buyers
and models learn, Optimize the assets that can be extracted and trusted, and Win with
conversion content and measurement.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the flow layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

Ahrefs found a 58% lower average CTR for position-one content when an AI Overview was
present in its December 2025 dataset.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Start Here", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Local SEO Overview](07-local-seo/local-seo-overview.md)
- [Prompt Library](09-prompts/README.md)

## Sources

- Search Engine Journal, State of SEO 2026 PDF and public landing page, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- Ahrefs, AI Overviews CTR update, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/01-framework/ai-search-surface-map.md -->

---
title: "AI Search Surface Map"
description: "AI Search Surface Map"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - framework
  - ai-search
---

![AI Search Surface Map](../../assets/diagrams/flow-v4-surface-map.png)

# AI Search Surface Map

## What This Is

AI search visibility now depends on how a brand is described across search results,
websites, profiles, communities, citations, videos, and business records.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the framework layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

Ahrefs found a 58% lower average CTR for position-one content when an AI Overview was
present in its December 2025 dataset.

Pew Research observed traditional-result clicks on 8% of Google visits with an AI summary,
compared with 15% without one.

Semrush and Datos found a zero-click-rate shift from 33.75% to 31.53% after AI Overviews
appeared for the same keywords; use that as a study-specific result, not a universal rule.

seoClarity found that 25% of top cited ChatGPT URLs had no Google organic visibility in its
cited-page sample.

BrightLocal reported 45% of consumers use generative AI tools for local business
recommendations, while business websites, business mentions, and directories appeared as
ChatGPT Search local sources at 58%, 27%, and 15%.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "AI Search Surface Map", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [Start Here](../00-START-HERE.md)
- [AI Search Surface Map](ai-search-surface-map.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- Ahrefs, AI Overviews CTR update, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- Pew Research Center AI summary click study, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- Semrush/Datos AI Overviews study, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- seoClarity, ChatGPT cited-page analysis, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- BrightLocal local SEO statistics, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/01-framework/flow-framework.md -->

---
title: "FLOW Framework"
description: "FLOW Framework"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - framework
---

![FLOW Operating Loop](../../assets/diagrams/flow-v4-flow-loop.png)

# FLOW Framework

## What This Is

FLOW is a search-and-conversion loop for 2026 discovery. It treats rankings, AI citations,
local visibility, and sales evidence as connected surfaces rather than separate channels.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the framework layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

Ahrefs found a 58% lower average CTR for position-one content when an AI Overview was
present in its December 2025 dataset.

seoClarity found that 25% of top cited ChatGPT URLs had no Google organic visibility in its
cited-page sample.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "FLOW Framework", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [Start Here](../00-START-HERE.md)
- [AI Search Surface Map](ai-search-surface-map.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

- Ahrefs, AI Overviews CTR update, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- seoClarity, ChatGPT cited-page analysis, retrieved 2026-04-25.

---
title: "Independence and Attribution"
description: "Independence and Attribution"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - framework
  - attribution
---

# Independence and Attribution

## What This Is

This public repository is written as an independent, evidence-led edition. Private notes
helped identify candidate concepts, but public prose, examples, prompts, and claims are re-
derived from independent sources.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the framework layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026



Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Independence and Attribution", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [Start Here](../00-START-HERE.md)
- [AI Search Surface Map](ai-search-surface-map.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- Local SEO Knowledge Base, private source document, public logic rewritten for this repository.
- 2025-2026 PDF source set listed in the bibliography.
- Search Engine Journal, State of SEO 2026 PDF and public landing page, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/02-foundations/search-intent-and-eeat.md -->

---
title: "Search Intent and E-E-A-T"
description: "Search Intent and E-E-A-T"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - foundations
---

# Search Intent and E-E-A-T

## What This Is

Intent analysis starts with the job behind a query, then adds evidence of experience,
expertise, authoritativeness, and trust so humans and AI systems can evaluate the answer.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the foundations layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026



Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Search Intent and E-E-A-T", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- Search Engine Journal, State of SEO 2026 PDF and public landing page, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- Local SEO Knowledge Base, private source document, public logic rewritten for this repository.


<!-- Source: docs/02-foundations/technical-and-schema-foundations.md -->

title: "Technical and Schema Foundations"

  - foundations

---

![Technical Clarity Stack](../../assets/diagrams/flow-v4-technical-stack.png)

# Technical and Schema Foundations

## What This Is

Technical SEO makes content available, understandable, and eligible for rich interpretation.
Schema is not a ranking shortcut; it is a clarity layer for entities, offers, services,
reviews, and organizations.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the foundations layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026



Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Technical and Schema Foundations", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

- 2025-2026 PDF source set listed in the bibliography.


<!-- Source: docs/03-find/audience-avatar-and-local-intent.md -->

---
title: "Audience Avatar and Local Intent"
description: "Audience Avatar and Local Intent"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - find
  - local
---

# Audience Avatar and Local Intent

## What This Is

Audience work becomes useful when it records real constraints: buyer role, geography,
urgency, budget sensitivity, objections, and the phrases people use before they trust a
provider.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the find layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

BrightLocal reported 45% of consumers use generative AI tools for local business
recommendations, while business websites, business mentions, and directories appeared as
ChatGPT Search local sources at 58%, 27%, and 15%.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.
- For local work, reconcile website facts, profile facts, citations, and review language before creating new pages.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Audience Avatar and Local Intent", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- Local SEO Knowledge Base, private source document, public logic rewritten for this repository.
- BrightLocal local SEO statistics, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/03-find/keyword-research.md -->

---
title: "Keyword Research"
description: "Keyword Research"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - find
---

# Keyword Research

## What This Is

Keyword research is the disciplined inventory of demand language across classic search,
local search, AI answers, and community surfaces.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the find layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026



Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Keyword Research", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- Local SEO Knowledge Base, private source document, public logic rewritten for this repository.
- Search Engine Journal, State of SEO 2026 PDF and public landing page, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/03-find/query-fan-out-and-topical-relevance.md -->

---
title: "Query Fan-Out and Topical Relevance"
description: "Query Fan-Out and Topical Relevance"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - find
  - ai-search
---

![Query Fan-Out](../../assets/diagrams/flow-v4-query-fanout.png)

# Query Fan-Out and Topical Relevance

## What This Is

Modern search systems fan a topic into adjacent questions, comparisons, definitions,
entities, and local modifiers. Topical relevance is built by answering the cluster with
clear evidence.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the find layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

Ahrefs found a 58% lower average CTR for position-one content when an AI Overview was
present in its December 2025 dataset.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Query Fan-Out and Topical Relevance", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- Search Engine Journal, State of SEO 2026 PDF and public landing page, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- Ahrefs, AI Overviews CTR update, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/04-leverage/distributed-presence.md -->

---
title: "Distributed Presence"
description: "Distributed Presence"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - leverage
---

![Distributed Presence](../../assets/diagrams/flow-v4-distributed-presence.png)

# Distributed Presence

## What This Is

Leverage is the work of making a brand findable and corroborated where search engines,
buyers, and AI systems gather evidence outside the owned site.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the leverage layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

BrightLocal reported 45% of consumers use generative AI tools for local business
recommendations, while business websites, business mentions, and directories appeared as
ChatGPT Search local sources at 58%, 27%, and 15%.

seoClarity found that 25% of top cited ChatGPT URLs had no Google organic visibility in its
cited-page sample.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.
- For off-site work, prioritize places that can corroborate the brand or expose buyer language, not empty link volume.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Distributed Presence", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- BrightLocal local SEO statistics, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- seoClarity, ChatGPT cited-page analysis, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/04-leverage/local-citations-and-links.md -->

---
title: "Local Citations and Links"
description: "Local Citations and Links"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - leverage
  - local
---

# Local Citations and Links

## What This Is

Citations and local links help search systems reconcile who a business is, where it
operates, and why nearby customers might trust it.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the leverage layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

BrightLocal reported 45% of consumers use generative AI tools for local business
recommendations, while business websites, business mentions, and directories appeared as
ChatGPT Search local sources at 58%, 27%, and 15%.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.
- For local work, reconcile website facts, profile facts, citations, and review language before creating new pages.
- For off-site work, prioritize places that can corroborate the brand or expose buyer language, not empty link volume.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Local Citations and Links", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- Local SEO Knowledge Base, private source document, public logic rewritten for this repository.
- BrightLocal local SEO statistics, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/04-leverage/reddit-linkedin-and-community.md -->

title: "Reddit, LinkedIn, and Community"

  - leverage
  - community
---

# Reddit, LinkedIn, and Community

## What This Is

Community surfaces matter because they hold language, objections, comparisons, and use cases
that formal brand pages often miss.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the leverage layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

seoClarity found that 25% of top cited ChatGPT URLs had no Google organic visibility in its
cited-page sample.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.
- For off-site work, prioritize places that can corroborate the brand or expose buyer language, not empty link volume.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Reddit, LinkedIn, and Community", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)

<!-- Source: docs/05-optimize/content-and-ctr-optimization.md -->

---
title: "Content and CTR Optimization"
description: "Content and CTR Optimization"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - optimize
---

# Content and CTR Optimization

## What This Is

Content optimization improves the page a searcher already sees: titles, descriptions, answer
order, proof, internal links, freshness, and conversion clarity.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the optimize layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

Ahrefs found a 58% lower average CTR for position-one content when an AI Overview was
present in its December 2025 dataset.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Content and CTR Optimization", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)
