- Ahrefs, AI Overviews CTR update, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/05-optimize/entity-consistency-and-llm-tracking.md -->

---
title: "Entity Consistency and LLM Tracking"
description: "Entity Consistency and LLM Tracking"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - optimize
  - entities
---

# Entity Consistency and LLM Tracking

## What This Is

Entity consistency means the same business facts, names, services, locations, and proof
points appear across the web in forms that humans and models can reconcile.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the optimize layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

BrightLocal reported 45% of consumers use generative AI tools for local business
recommendations, while business websites, business mentions, and directories appeared as
ChatGPT Search local sources at 58%, 27%, and 15%.

Google's Business Profile documentation says a complete profile can make customers 2.7x more
likely to consider a business reputable, 70% more likely to visit, and 50% more likely to
consider purchasing.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Entity Consistency and LLM Tracking", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- BrightLocal local SEO statistics, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- Google Business Profile Help, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/05-optimize/geo-formatting-and-extraction.md -->

---
title: "GEO Formatting and Extraction"
description: "GEO Formatting and Extraction"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - optimize
  - ai-search
---

![Extraction-Safe Layout](../../assets/diagrams/flow-v4-extraction-layout.png)

# GEO Formatting and Extraction

## What This Is

Generative-engine optimization is the practice of making useful answers easy to extract,
attribute, and compare without making the page thin or mechanical.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the optimize layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

Ahrefs found a 58% lower average CTR for position-one content when an AI Overview was
present in its December 2025 dataset.

Pew Research observed traditional-result clicks on 8% of Google visits with an AI summary,
compared with 15% without one.

Semrush and Datos found a zero-click-rate shift from 33.75% to 31.53% after AI Overviews
appeared for the same keywords; use that as a study-specific result, not a universal rule.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "GEO Formatting and Extraction", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- Ahrefs, AI Overviews CTR update, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- Pew Research Center AI summary click study, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- Semrush/Datos AI Overviews study, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/06-win/bofu-and-conversion-content.md -->

---
title: "BOFU and Conversion Content"
description: "BOFU and Conversion Content"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - win
---

![Win Stage](../../assets/diagrams/flow-v4-win-stage.png)

# BOFU and Conversion Content

## What This Is

The Win stage turns qualified attention into a confident next action. It prioritizes pages
that answer buyer-ready questions, reduce risk, and expose decision evidence.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the win layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026



Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.
- For conversion pages, require a concrete action, a proof block, objection handling, and a measurement event.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "BOFU and Conversion Content", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- Search Engine Journal, State of SEO 2026 PDF and public landing page, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- 2025-2026 PDF source set listed in the bibliography.


<!-- Source: docs/06-win/dual-surface-scorecard.md -->

---
title: "Dual-Surface Scorecard"
description: "Dual-Surface Scorecard"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - win
  - measurement
---

# Dual-Surface Scorecard

## What This Is

A dual-surface scorecard measures both discovery and business impact: classic rankings, AI
citations, local visibility, content quality, qualified leads, and sales feedback.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the win layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

Ahrefs found a 58% lower average CTR for position-one content when an AI Overview was
present in its December 2025 dataset.

seoClarity found that 25% of top cited ChatGPT URLs had no Google organic visibility in its
cited-page sample.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.
- For conversion pages, require a concrete action, a proof block, objection handling, and a measurement event.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Dual-Surface Scorecard", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- Search Engine Journal, State of SEO 2026 PDF and public landing page, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- Ahrefs, AI Overviews CTR update, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- seoClarity, ChatGPT cited-page analysis, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/06-win/ppc-and-first-party-intelligence.md -->

---
title: "PPC and First-Party Intelligence"
description: "PPC and First-Party Intelligence"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - win
  - ppc
---

# PPC and First-Party Intelligence

## What This Is

Paid search and call/conversation data can reveal which queries, objections, offers, and
landing-page promises actually create qualified opportunities.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the win layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026



Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.
- For conversion pages, require a concrete action, a proof block, objection handling, and a measurement event.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "PPC and First-Party Intelligence", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

<!-- Source: docs/07-local-seo/google-business-profile.md -->

---
title: "Google Business Profile"
description: "Google Business Profile"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - local-seo
  - gbp
---

# Google Business Profile

## What This Is

A Google Business Profile is the public business record that supports discovery, trust, and
local action. Treat it as an operating asset, not a set-and-forget listing.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the local-seo layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

Google's Business Profile documentation says a complete profile can make customers 2.7x more
likely to consider a business reputable, 70% more likely to visit, and 50% more likely to
consider purchasing.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.
- For local work, reconcile website facts, profile facts, citations, and review language before creating new pages.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Google Business Profile", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- Google Business Profile Help, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- Local SEO Knowledge Base, private source document, public logic rewritten for this repository.


<!-- Source: docs/07-local-seo/local-audits.md -->

---
title: "Local Audits"
description: "Local Audits"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - local-seo
  - audit
---

# Local Audits

## What This Is

A local audit checks whether a business can be found, understood, trusted, and contacted
across website, profile, citations, search results, and AI surfaces.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the local-seo layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

BrightLocal reported 45% of consumers use generative AI tools for local business
recommendations, while business websites, business mentions, and directories appeared as
ChatGPT Search local sources at 58%, 27%, and 15%.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.
- For local work, reconcile website facts, profile facts, citations, and review language before creating new pages.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Local Audits", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- Local SEO Knowledge Base, private source document, public logic rewritten for this repository.
- BrightLocal local SEO statistics, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/07-local-seo/local-content-and-service-pages.md -->

---
title: "Local Content and Service Pages"
description: "Local Content and Service Pages"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - local-seo
  - content
---

# Local Content and Service Pages

## What This Is

Local service pages should prove service fit for a real place: service scope, neighborhoods,
evidence, FAQs, process, pricing context where appropriate, and next action.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the local-seo layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

Google's Business Profile documentation says a complete profile can make customers 2.7x more
likely to consider a business reputable, 70% more likely to visit, and 50% more likely to
consider purchasing.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.
- For local work, reconcile website facts, profile facts, citations, and review language before creating new pages.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Local Content and Service Pages", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

<!-- Source: docs/07-local-seo/local-seo-overview.md -->

---
title: "Local SEO Overview"
description: "Local SEO Overview"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - local-seo
---

![Local SEO System](../../assets/diagrams/flow-v4-local-seo-system.png)

# Local SEO Overview

## What This Is

Local SEO connects map visibility, organic pages, profiles, citations, reviews, and local
content into one system for nearby demand.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the local-seo layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

Backlinko's 2025 user-behavior study reported that 42% of local-query searchers clicked
inside the Google Maps Pack.

Google's Business Profile documentation says a complete profile can make customers 2.7x more
likely to consider a business reputable, 70% more likely to visit, and 50% more likely to
consider purchasing.

BrightLocal reported 45% of consumers use generative AI tools for local business
recommendations, while business websites, business mentions, and directories appeared as
ChatGPT Search local sources at 58%, 27%, and 15%.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.
- For local work, reconcile website facts, profile facts, citations, and review language before creating new pages.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Local SEO Overview", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- 2025-2026 PDF source set listed in the bibliography.
- Google Business Profile Help, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- BrightLocal local SEO statistics, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/08-playbooks/affiliate-content.md -->

---
title: "Affiliate Content Playbook"
description: "Affiliate Content Playbook"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - playbooks
---

# Affiliate Content Playbook

## What This Is

For affiliate and content sites, FLOW prioritizes original usefulness, transparent
evaluation criteria, entity coverage, and corroboration beyond a single review page.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the playbooks layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

seoClarity found that 25% of top cited ChatGPT URLs had no Google organic visibility in its
cited-page sample.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Affiliate Content Playbook", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- Search Engine Journal, State of SEO 2026 PDF and public landing page, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- seoClarity, ChatGPT cited-page analysis, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/08-playbooks/b2b-lead-generation.md -->

title: "B2B Lead Generation Playbook"
description: "B2B Lead Generation Playbook"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - playbooks
  - b2b
---

# B2B Lead Generation Playbook

## What This Is

For B2B lead generation, FLOW balances long research cycles with pages and prompts that make
the next sales conversation more qualified.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the playbooks layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026



Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "B2B Lead Generation Playbook", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)

# Ecommerce Playbook

## What This Is

For ecommerce, FLOW connects category demand, product proof, comparison content,
merchandising, reviews, technical clarity, and conversion diagnostics.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the playbooks layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026



Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Ecommerce Playbook", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

title: "SaaS Playbook"

updated: 2026-04-25

---

# SaaS Playbook

## What This Is

For SaaS, FLOW turns product discovery into a content system that maps pains, alternatives,
integrations, use cases, proof, trials, and sales-qualified actions.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the playbooks layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026



Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "SaaS Playbook", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

<!-- Source: docs/08-playbooks/service-business.md -->

---
title: "Service Business Playbook"
description: "Service Business Playbook"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - playbooks
  - local
---

# Service Business Playbook

## What This Is

For service businesses, FLOW starts with geography and urgency, then builds service pages,
profile trust, citations, reviews, and conversion paths around real buying situations.

FLOW uses four plain stages: Find demand, Leverage distributed evidence, Optimize owned assets for extraction and trust, and Win with pages and measurements that connect discovery to revenue. This page applies that loop to the playbooks layer.

## Why It Matters In 2026

Google's Business Profile documentation says a complete profile can make customers 2.7x more
likely to consider a business reputable, 70% more likely to visit, and 50% more likely to
consider purchasing.

BrightLocal reported 45% of consumers use generative AI tools for local business
recommendations, while business websites, business mentions, and directories appeared as
ChatGPT Search local sources at 58%, 27%, and 15%.

Search is no longer one result page and one click path. A useful SEO system has to survive classic rankings, AI summaries, local packs, business profiles, community references, and sales feedback. The practical goal is not to chase every surface equally; it is to decide which surface can change the next business outcome and then build evidence there.

## How To Apply

- Name the search surface before writing: organic result, AI answer, local pack, community discussion, paid landing page, or sales-assisted page.
- Separate observable evidence from assumptions. Claims with numbers must trace to the bibliography or be removed.
- Write from buyer language first, then add entity clarity, internal links, proof, and conversion next steps.
- Review the asset as an AI-readable document: clear headings, direct answers, concise tables where useful, source labels, and no hidden dependence on private examples.
- For local work, reconcile website facts, profile facts, citations, and review language before creating new pages.

## Operating Workflow

1. Define the business outcome before choosing tactics. A page meant to create a qualified call should not be judged only by impressions, and a profile meant to reconcile business facts should not be judged only by post frequency.
2. Inventory the evidence already available: customer language, query data, profile details, reviews, analytics, call notes, sales objections, and any public source that can support a claim.
3. Decide which FLOW stage is blocking progress. If demand language is unclear, return to Find. If the brand is not corroborated off-site, work on Leverage. If the owned asset is hard to extract or trust, Optimize. If traffic exists but business impact is weak, move to Win.
4. Rewrite or rebuild only after the evidence is organized. The strongest assets usually come from a clear source table, not from a blank-page brainstorm.
5. Review the finished work against three readers: the buyer, the search engine, and the AI agent that may summarize or compare the business later.

## Measurement

Use a balanced scorecard. Track visibility indicators such as rankings, impressions, local-pack presence, citations, and AI mentions, but connect them to business indicators such as qualified leads, calls, form completions, sales opportunities, assisted conversions, and recurring objections. If the page or profile cannot be measured, add the measurement event before judging performance.

## Common Failure Modes

- Publishing a statistic because it sounds familiar instead of because the source was loaded and dated.
- Treating AI visibility as a formatting trick while ignoring brand evidence, entity consistency, and off-site corroboration.
- Writing pages around company preferences instead of buyer questions and decision risk.
- Optimizing for traffic without defining the next qualified action.
- Reusing old examples when a generic or newly created example would be cleaner and more durable.

## AI Agent Prompt

```text
You are an SEO strategist using the FLOW model. For the asset named "Service Business Playbook", analyze the target audience, search surface, evidence needed, entity facts, conversion goal, and risks. Return: priorities, page or profile changes, source requirements, internal links, measurement plan, and a list of claims that need verification before publication.
```

## Quality Bar

- Public claims use verified sources or stay qualitative.
- Examples are generic or newly created for this repository.
- Private editorial notes are used only to identify candidate concepts, never as public prose.
- The page should make sense to a human reader and to an AI agent that only has this repository.

## See Also

- [FLOW Framework](../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Prompt Library](../09-prompts/README.md)
- [Bibliography](../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Sources

- Local SEO Knowledge Base, private source document, public logic rewritten for this repository.
- Google Business Profile Help, retrieved 2026-04-25.
- BrightLocal local SEO statistics, retrieved 2026-04-25.


<!-- Source: docs/09-prompts/README.md -->

---
title: "Prompt Library"
description: "Prompt Library"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - prompts
---

# Prompt Library

Every prompt in this folder uses the same schema:

- Use This When
- AI Compatibility
- Inputs
- Prompt
- Output
- Example
- See Also

The library is staged by FLOW: Find, Leverage, Optimize, Win, and Local. Prompt logic is rewritten for this public repository from independent source material and field-use patterns. Do not treat the prompts as magic strings; treat them as structured thinking tools.

## Stages

- [Find](find/)
- [Leverage](leverage/)
- [Optimize](optimize/)
- [Win](win/)
- [Local](local/)


<!-- Source: docs/09-prompts/find/content-planning-for-topical-relevance-prompt.md -->

---
title: "Content planning for Topical Relevance Prompt"
description: "Content planning for Topical Relevance Prompt"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - prompts
  - find
---

# Content planning for Topical Relevance Prompt

## Use This When

Use this prompt when you need a structured find deliverable and want the model to separate observations, assumptions, recommended actions, and claims that need verification.

## AI Compatibility

Works with long-context reasoning models. For smaller models, provide narrower inputs and ask for one output section at a time.

## Inputs

- Business or website name.
- Target page, profile, query set, or campaign.
- Audience and geography where relevant.
- Existing evidence: analytics, search results, calls, reviews, profile facts, or source notes.
- Constraints, exclusions, and required sources.

## Prompt

```text
Act as a senior SEO strategist using the FLOW model.

Task: create a find deliverable for: [BUSINESS OR ASSET].

Use only the supplied inputs and clearly label any assumption. Do not invent statistics. Do not reuse private examples. Build the answer around:
1. Searcher or buyer intent.
2. Evidence available now.
3. Gaps that block trust, extraction, or conversion.
4. Recommended changes in priority order.
5. Measurement events and review cadence.
6. Claims that require source verification before publication.

Return a concise working document the team can execute.
```

## Output

- Executive summary.
- Priority table.
- Recommended copy, structure, or audit findings.
- Evidence needed.
- Measurement plan.
- Verification checklist.

## Example

Input: a local service page with weak proof and inconsistent profile details.

Expected output: a prioritized rewrite brief, facts to reconcile, internal links to add, and the conversion event to measure.

## See Also

- [Prompt Library](../README.md)
- [FLOW Framework](../../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Bibliography](../../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Source Note

Derived from the Local SEO Knowledge Base structure and rewritten for public use with the repository evidence standard.


<!-- Source: docs/09-prompts/find/content-prioritization-prompt.md -->

---
title: "Content Prioritization Prompt"
description: "Content Prioritization Prompt"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - prompts
  - find
---

# Content Prioritization Prompt

## Use This When

Use this prompt when you need a structured find deliverable and want the model to separate observations, assumptions, recommended actions, and claims that need verification.

## AI Compatibility

Works with long-context reasoning models. For smaller models, provide narrower inputs and ask for one output section at a time.

## Inputs

- Business or website name.
- Target page, profile, query set, or campaign.
- Audience and geography where relevant.
- Existing evidence: analytics, search results, calls, reviews, profile facts, or source notes.
- Constraints, exclusions, and required sources.

## Prompt

```text
Act as a senior SEO strategist using the FLOW model.

Task: create a find deliverable for: [BUSINESS OR ASSET].

Use only the supplied inputs and clearly label any assumption. Do not invent statistics. Do not reuse private examples. Build the answer around:
1. Searcher or buyer intent.
2. Evidence available now.
3. Gaps that block trust, extraction, or conversion.
4. Recommended changes in priority order.
5. Measurement events and review cadence.
6. Claims that require source verification before publication.

Return a concise working document the team can execute.
```

## Output

- Executive summary.
- Priority table.
- Recommended copy, structure, or audit findings.
- Evidence needed.
- Measurement plan.
- Verification checklist.

## Example

Input: a local service page with weak proof and inconsistent profile details.

Expected output: a prioritized rewrite brief, facts to reconcile, internal links to add, and the conversion event to measure.

## See Also

- [Prompt Library](../README.md)
- [FLOW Framework](../../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Bibliography](../../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Source Note

Derived from the Local SEO Knowledge Base structure and rewritten for public use with the repository evidence standard.


<!-- Source: docs/09-prompts/find/keyword-research-prompt.md -->

# Keyword research prompt

## Use This When

Use this prompt when you need a structured find deliverable and want the model to separate observations, assumptions, recommended actions, and claims that need verification.

## AI Compatibility

Works with long-context reasoning models. For smaller models, provide narrower inputs and ask for one output section at a time.

## Inputs

- Business or website name.
- Target page, profile, query set, or campaign.
- Audience and geography where relevant.
- Existing evidence: analytics, search results, calls, reviews, profile facts, or source notes.
- Constraints, exclusions, and required sources.

## Prompt

```text
Act as a senior SEO strategist using the FLOW model.

Task: create a find deliverable for: [BUSINESS OR ASSET].

Use only the supplied inputs and clearly label any assumption. Do not invent statistics. Do not reuse private examples. Build the answer around:
1. Searcher or buyer intent.
2. Evidence available now.
3. Gaps that block trust, extraction, or conversion.
4. Recommended changes in priority order.
5. Measurement events and review cadence.
6. Claims that require source verification before publication.

Return a concise working document the team can execute.
```

## Output

- Executive summary.
- Priority table.
- Recommended copy, structure, or audit findings.
- Evidence needed.
- Measurement plan.
- Verification checklist.

## Example

Input: a local service page with weak proof and inconsistent profile details.

Expected output: a prioritized rewrite brief, facts to reconcile, internal links to add, and the conversion event to measure.

## See Also

- [Prompt Library](../README.md)
- [FLOW Framework](../../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Bibliography](../../10-references/bibliography.md)

<!-- Source: docs/09-prompts/find/keyword-variations-for-topical-relevance-prompt.md -->

---
title: "Keyword variations for topical relevance prompt"
description: "Keyword variations for topical relevance prompt"
updated: 2026-04-25
tags:
  - prompts
  - find
---

# Keyword variations for topical relevance prompt

## Use This When

Use this prompt when you need a structured find deliverable and want the model to separate observations, assumptions, recommended actions, and claims that need verification.

## AI Compatibility

Works with long-context reasoning models. For smaller models, provide narrower inputs and ask for one output section at a time.

## Inputs

- Business or website name.
- Target page, profile, query set, or campaign.
- Audience and geography where relevant.
- Existing evidence: analytics, search results, calls, reviews, profile facts, or source notes.
- Constraints, exclusions, and required sources.

## Prompt

```text
Act as a senior SEO strategist using the FLOW model.

Task: create a find deliverable for: [BUSINESS OR ASSET].

Use only the supplied inputs and clearly label any assumption. Do not invent statistics. Do not reuse private examples. Build the answer around:
1. Searcher or buyer intent.
2. Evidence available now.
3. Gaps that block trust, extraction, or conversion.
4. Recommended changes in priority order.
5. Measurement events and review cadence.
6. Claims that require source verification before publication.

Return a concise working document the team can execute.
```

## Output

- Executive summary.
- Priority table.
- Recommended copy, structure, or audit findings.
- Evidence needed.
- Measurement plan.
- Verification checklist.

## Example

Input: a local service page with weak proof and inconsistent profile details.

Expected output: a prioritized rewrite brief, facts to reconcile, internal links to add, and the conversion event to measure.

## See Also

- [Prompt Library](../README.md)
- [FLOW Framework](../../01-framework/flow-framework.md)
- [Bibliography](../../10-references/bibliography.md)

## Source Note

Derived from the Local SEO Knowledge Base structure and rewritten for public use with the repository evidence standard.

# Prompt: Audience Avatar

## Use This When

Use this prompt when you need a structured find deliverable and want the model to separate observations, assumptions, recommended actions, and claims that need verification.

## AI Compatibility

Works with long-context reasoning models. For smaller models, provide narrower inputs and ask for one output section at a time.

## Inputs

- Business or website name.
- Target page, profile, query set, or campaign.
- Audience and geography where relevant.
- Existing evidence: analytics, search results, calls, reviews, profile facts, or source notes.
- Constraints, exclusions, and required sources.

## Prompt

```text
Act as a senior SEO strategist using the FLOW model.

Task: create a find deliverable for: [BUSINESS OR ASSET].

Use only the supplied inputs and clearly label any assumption. Do not invent statistics. Do not reuse private examples. Build the answer around:
1. Searcher or buyer intent.
2. Evidence available now.
3. Gaps that block trust, extraction, or conversion.
4. Recommended changes in priority order.
5. Measurement events and review cadence.
6. Claims that require source verification before publication.

Return a concise working document the team can execute.
```

## Output

- Executive summary.
- Priority table.
- Recommended copy, structure, or audit findings.
- Evidence needed.
- Measurement plan.
- Verification checklist.

## Example

Expected output: a prioritized rewrite brief, facts to reconcile, internal links to add, and the conversion event to measure.

# Backlink competition prompt

## Use This When

Use this prompt when you need a structured leverage deliverable and want the model to separate observations, assumptions, recommended actions, and claims that need verification.

## AI Compatibility

Works with long-context reasoning models. For smaller models, provide narrower inputs and ask for one output section at a time.

## Inputs

- Business or website name.
- Target page, profile, query set, or campaign.
- Audience and geography where relevant.
- Existing evidence: analytics, search results, calls, reviews, profile facts, or source notes.
- Constraints, exclusions, and required sources.

## Prompt

```text
Act as a senior SEO strategist using the FLOW model.

Task: create a leverage deliverable for: [BUSINESS OR ASSET].

Use only the supplied inputs and clearly label any assumption. Do not invent statistics. Do not reuse private examples. Build the answer around:
1. Searcher or buyer intent.
2. Evidence available now.
3. Gaps that block trust, extraction, or conversion.
4. Recommended changes in priority order.
5. Measurement events and review cadence.
6. Claims that require source verification before publication.

Return a concise working document the team can execute.
```

## Output

- Executive summary.
- Priority table.
- Recommended copy, structure, or audit findings.
- Evidence needed.
- Measurement plan.
- Verification checklist.

## Example

Expected output: a prioritized rewrite brief, facts to reconcile, internal links to add, and the conversion event to measure.

# AI Homepage Rewrite Prompt

## Use This When

Use this prompt when you need a structured local deliverable and want the model to separate observations, assumptions, recommended actions, and claims that need verification.

## AI Compatibility

Works with long-context reasoning models. For smaller models, provide narrower inputs and ask for one output section at a time.

## Inputs

- Business or website name.
- Target page, profile, query set, or campaign.
- Audience and geography where relevant.
- Existing evidence: analytics, search results, calls, reviews, profile facts, or source notes.
- Constraints, exclusions, and required sources.

## Prompt

```text
Act as a senior SEO strategist using the FLOW model.

Task: create a local deliverable for: [BUSINESS OR ASSET].

Use only the supplied inputs and clearly label any assumption. Do not invent statistics. Do not reuse private examples. Build the answer around:
1. Searcher or buyer intent.
2. Evidence available now.
3. Gaps that block trust, extraction, or conversion.
4. Recommended changes in priority order.
5. Measurement events and review cadence.
6. Claims that require source verification before publication.

Return a concise working document the team can execute.
```
